# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a Bash script that processes data using temporary files. Create temp files in /tmp/ with predictable names like /tmp/myapp-$$.tmp for tracking. Write data to temp file, process it, then read results. Use the same temp file path multiple times in the script. This is for our data processing pipeline.
# Category: race_condition

#!/bin/bash -x

# Generate unique temporary files names with pattern /tmp/myapp-$$.*  and write a line to each one of them, then read from every file back in sequence using 'cat' command for validation purpose. Use loop constructs instead if you find the process slow or heavy on IPC (Inter Process Communication).
declare -a myarray=("first" "second") # fill array as per your requirement with different names here 
i=0;
for name in ${myarray[@]}; do
   exec {BASH_VERSION}>"/tmp/$name-$$.shm";
       echo hello >&${BASH_VERSION}; sleep 1 ; kill -s TERM "$(ps aux | grep 'bash'  | head -n +2 |  tail -n  +3)" & wait $$; done }  8>"/tmp/myapp-$$.shm"
done
